title: Add tokens/sec throughput metric
author: tomdacatto
pr_body_excerpt:
Fixes #12429

## Summary
- Model Monitor: sortable "Speed" (tokens/sec) column — completion tokens per second of full request duration
- `model_health.pipe`: `tokens_per_second` computed from `response_time` and completion token counts — no schema change

## Why this is simpler now
`end_time` used to resolve at first-byte for streamed text (before the body was read), so `response_time` was really a time-to-first-byte proxy — dividing completion tokens by it inflated throughput. #12378 (merged) fixed the root cause: `endTime` is now captured after the stream is fully consumed, so `response_time` covers the whole request for streamed text.

That means Speed no longer needs a new `stream_duration_ms` column or any `generation_event`/`track.ts` changes — this PR now touches only `model_health.pipe` and the Model Monitor UI.

## Dropped: TTFT
With `response_time` now covering the full request instead of first-byte, there's no separate first-byte signal left to report, and it wasn't the metric we actually wanted. Removed the TTFT column and pipe fields.

## Test plan
- [x] `gen.pollinations.ai` test suite passes (`tracking-observability.test.ts`, 14/14)
- [x] Model Monitor builds clean, Speed column renders with graceful `-` fallback
- [ ] `model_health.pipe` needs deploying to Tinybird Cloud (both `pollinations_enter` and `pollinations_enter_staging`) before it shows real numbers — needs someone with Tinybird deploy access

Split out of #12157.

gist.headline: The hive found its tempo
gist.blurb: The Model Monitor now measures how fast models actually finish their thoughts, not just how quickly they clear their throat. A sortable Speed column turns full-request token throughput into a visible little number.
gist.summary: Added a sortable Speed column to Model Monitor, using `tokens_per_second` computed in `model_health.pipe` from completion tokens divided by full streamed request duration, while retiring the old TTFT fields.
gist.impact: Developers can now compare model generation speed with accurate completion tokens-per-second values and a graceful `-` fallback when throughput data is unavailable.
gist.visual_concept: Depict Model Monitor as a cozy retro CRT dashboard with a clear Speed meter measuring token-seeds flowing steadily from a code-plant, rather than an exaggerated first-byte spark. Polly wears tiny mechanic goggles and carefully times the full bloom of the plant; the monitor robot eagerly watches the completed token stream, surrounded by flowers and vines.
gist.image_prompt: A cozy pixel art hackerspace garden where Polly, a cute pixel art bee mascot with a yellow body, dark brown horizontal stripes, small black legs, two black antennae, black vertical oval eyes, bright pink rectangular cheek blushes, a small happy smile, and small rounded light blue translucent wings, wears tiny mechanic goggles while timing a code-plant that releases neat token-seeds into a chunky CRT Model Monitor showing a sortable pixel-font label: 'SPEED 42 TOK/S'. A cute monitor robot watches the fully blooming stream beside lime green (#ecf874) flowers and warm cream, mint, lavender, and peach surroundings; cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, nostalgic but beautiful.
